mapping.a_element: lognormal fluctuations of energy dissipation and related quantities across segments
mapping.b_element: power-law scaling of the two-point correlation function of energy dissipation

mapping.a_element: multiplicative stochastic process driving fluctuations across scales
mapping.b_element: intermittency exponent mu characterizing scaling in the inertial range

mapping.a_element: segment length within the range of large scales with weak velocity correlations
mapping.b_element: separation distances in the inertial range where power-law scaling holds

brief_justification: Paper A identifies a lognormal distribution of fluctuations in turbulence quantities driven by a multiplicative stochastic process, while Paper B focuses on power-law scaling and an intermittency exponent in the correlation function of energy dissipation. Though both address turbulence and energy dissipation, the core mechanisms—lognormality from multiplicative processes versus power-law scaling from surrogacy effects—are distinct and not role-for-role equivalent.
